title: 1.1: Replaces thd_charset() wrapper calls with direct THD::charset() member access at every InnoDB call site that resolves charset for foreign keys and INFORMATION_SCHEMA

annotations.description: Verifies that calls to the C-style thd_charset() service API wrapper are replaced with direct calls to the THD::charset() member function in InnoDB source files that resolve character set information for foreign key parsing and INFORMATION_SCHEMA population.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: dict0dict.cc replaces thd_charset(trx->mysql_thd) with trx->mysql_thd->charset(); ha_innodb.cc replaces thd_charset(m_thd) with m_thd->charset(); handler0alter.cc replaces thd_charset(trx->mysql_thd) with trx->mysql_thd->charset(); trx0i_s.cc replaces thd_charset(trx->mysql_thd) with trx->mysql_thd->charset()
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the code goes through 'C-style thd_*() service API wrapper functions for things like ... accessing the charset' and asks to 'clean up this indirection ... so that THD access is more direct and readable.' [3: Logical Necessity] thd_charset() is a service API wrapper that adds indirection; replacing it with direct member access is the core refactoring requested. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must replace thd_charset() calls with direct THD charset() member access in InnoDB source files.

title: 1.2: Replaces thd_mark_transaction_to_rollback() calls with direct thd->mark_transaction_to_rollback() in InnoDB (ha_innodb.cc)

annotations.description: Verifies that the extern C thd_mark_transaction_to_rollback() call sites in ha_innodb.cc are replaced with direct thd->mark_transaction_to_rollback(all) member calls.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: ha_innodb.cc replaces thd_mark_transaction_to_rollback(thd, all) with thd->mark_transaction_to_rollback(all) in innodb_transaction_abort and convert_error_code_to_mysql
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the code goes through 'C-style thd_*() service API wrapper functions for things like ... marking transactions for rollback' and asks to clean up the indirection in InnoDB. [3: Logical Necessity] InnoDB can include sql_class.h directly, so direct member access is the expected replacement. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must replace thd_mark_transaction_to_rollback() with direct thd->mark_transaction_to_rollback() in ha_innodb.cc.

title: 1.3: Replaces the extern C thd_mark_transaction_to_rollback() declaration in RocksDB (ha_rocksdb.cc) with a local static inline wrapper that calls thd->mark_transaction_to_rollback()

annotations.description: Verifies that the extern C declaration of thd_mark_transaction_to_rollback() in ha_rocksdb.cc is replaced with a local static inline wrapper that calls thd->mark_transaction_to_rollback(all). RocksDB cannot include sql_class.h directly, so a local wrapper is the expected approach rather than fully inlined member access.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: ha_rocksdb.cc replaces the extern C declaration with a static inline wrapper that calls thd->mark_transaction_to_rollback(all)
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks to clean up service API indirection in 'InnoDB and RocksDB storage engine code.' [3: Logical Necessity] RocksDB cannot include sql_class.h directly, so a local static inline wrapper that calls the THD member function is the expected approach to eliminate the extern C service API indirection. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must replace the extern C declaration in ha_rocksdb.cc with a local static inline wrapper.

title: 1.4: Replaces thd_is_strict_mode() wrapper calls with direct THD::is_strict_mode() member access in InnoDB foreign key validation and in-place ALTER TABLE preparation

annotations.description: Verifies that calls to the C-style thd_is_strict_mode() service API wrapper are replaced with direct calls to the THD::is_strict_mode() member function in InnoDB foreign key constraint validation and in-place ALTER TABLE preparation.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: dict0crea.cc replaces thd_is_strict_mode(trx->mysql_thd) with trx->mysql_thd->is_strict_mode(); handler0alter.cc replaces thd_is_strict_mode in 3 locations (prepare_inplace_alter_table_dict, prepare_inplace_alter_table) with direct member access
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the code goes through 'C-style thd_*() service API wrapper functions for things like ... checking strict mode' and asks to clean up this indirection. [3: Logical Necessity] thd_is_strict_mode() is called in multiple FK validation paths; all must be updated. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must replace thd_is_strict_mode() calls with direct THD member access in the InnoDB FK and ALTER TABLE code.

title: 1.5: Simplifies thd_mdl_context() usage by removing the static_cast and null-check indirection, accessing thd->mdl_context directly across all four InnoDB subsystems that touch MDL (dictionary, locking, purge, undo)

annotations.description: Verifies that the overly defensive pattern of calling thd_mdl_context(), casting the result to MDL_context*, and null-checking it is replaced with direct access to thd->mdl_context across all InnoDB source files that acquire or release MDL locks.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: dict0dict.cc simplifies mdl_release(), dict_acquire_mdl_shared(), dict_stats::open(); lock0lock.cc simplifies lock_table_children(); row0uins.cc simplifies row_undo_ins_remove_clust_rec(); trx0purge.cc simplifies trx_purge_close_tables(), close_and_reopen(), trx_purge_attach_undo_recs() - all by removing the static_cast<MDL_context*>(thd_mdl_context(thd)) pattern
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly states 'The thd_mdl_context() usage throughout the code is also overly defensive, casting and null checking a context that is always present on any valid THD.' [3: Logical Necessity] The MDL context is a direct member of THD and is always valid, so the cast and null check are unnecessary indirection. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must simplify thd_mdl_context() usage by accessing thd->mdl_context directly.

title: 1.6: Removes both InnoDB-specific helper wrappers thd_trx_is_read_only() and thd_trx_is_auto_commit() from the InnoDB handler implementation and their declarations from the shared InnoDB prototypes header

annotations.description: Verifies that the second-layer helper functions thd_trx_is_read_only() and thd_trx_is_auto_commit() are removed from ha_innodb.cc and their forward declarations are removed from ha_prototypes.h, with their logic inlined or restructured at the call site (such as trx_start_low in trx0trx.cc).
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: ha_innodb.cc removes both function definitions; ha_prototypes.h removes both declarations; trx0trx.cc restructures trx_start_low() to directly access thd->variables.option_bits, thd->lex->sql_command, and thd->tx_read_only
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly names these functions: 'InnoDB has its own second layer of helper functions in ha_innodb.cc like thd_trx_is_read_only() and thd_trx_is_auto_commit() that just delegate to other service calls, with declarations scattered across ha_innodb.h and ha_prototypes.h.' [3: Logical Necessity] These wrappers delegate to service API calls that themselves wrap THD members, creating a double layer of indirection. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must remove both wrapper functions and their declarations.

title: 1.7: Removes thd_has_edited_nontrans_tables() from the InnoDB handler and shared prototypes while preserving equivalent nontransactional-table-edit detection for deadlock victim selection

annotations.description: Verifies that thd_has_edited_nontrans_tables() is removed as a public helper function from ha_innodb.cc and ha_prototypes.h, but the equivalent check for nontransactional-table edits is preserved where it is actually used (the deadlock detection code in lock0lock.cc).
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: ha_innodb.cc removes the thd_has_edited_nontrans_tables() function definition; ha_prototypes.h removes its declaration; lock0lock.cc adds a static thd_has_edited_nontrans_tables() in the Deadlock namespace that directly accesses thd->transaction->all.modified_non_trans_table
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks to remove 'unnecessary wrappers and their declarations.' thd_has_edited_nontrans_tables() is an InnoDB-specific wrapper that delegates to thd_non_transactional_update(). [3: Logical Necessity] The deadlock detector requires this check to select the appropriate victim, so the functionality must be preserved in some form. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must remove the public wrapper while preserving the functionality for deadlock detection.

title: 1.8: Corrects thread identifier types from ulong to my_thread_id (or platform-equivalent) across all three surfaces (InnoDB lock structures, INFORMATION_SCHEMA trx row types, WSREP-related signatures)

annotations.description: Verifies that the incorrect ulong type used for thread identifiers is corrected to my_thread_id (or the platform-correct equivalent) in InnoDB structures and function signatures, including the i_s_trx_row_t struct, lock_wait_wsrep_kill() parameter, and wsrep_handle_lock_conflict() local variables.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: trx0i_s.h changes ulint trx_mysql_thread_id to my_thread_id; ha_innodb.cc changes lock_wait_wsrep_kill parameter from ulong to my_thread_id; lock0lock.cc changes lock_wait_wsrep_kill declaration and victim_id vector pair type from ulong to my_thread_id
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The thread ID handling uses the wrong type in several places, leading to mismatched format specifiers in debug and log output.' [3: Logical Necessity] Using ulong for a thread ID that is actually my_thread_id (unsigned long long) causes truncation on platforms where ulong is 32 bits. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must correct the thread identifier type to my_thread_id or equivalent.

title: 1.9: Updates thread identifier printf format specifiers in InnoDB debug and log output so width and signedness match my_thread_id without %ld/%lu mismatches

annotations.description: Verifies that printf-style format specifiers for thread identifiers are updated from %ld (or %lu) to %llu (or equivalent) in WSREP_DEBUG, fprintf, and DBUG_PRINT statements to match the corrected thread_id type and avoid undefined behavior.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: ha_innodb.cc changes %ld to %llu in multiple WSREP_DEBUG calls in write_row, update_row, delete_row, wsrep_append_key, wsrep_append_keys, and lock_wait_wsrep_kill; also changes DBUG_PRINT format string
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the wrong type leads to 'mismatched format specifiers in debug and log output.' [3: Logical Necessity] Format specifier mismatches cause undefined behavior and incorrect output. The format must match the actual type. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must update format specifiers to match the corrected thread_id type.

title: 1.10: Adds missing default cases to switch statements on enum values in the InnoDB handler code to eliminate compiler warnings

annotations.description: Verifies that switch statements on enum values in the InnoDB handler code that were missing default cases now have default: break; to suppress compiler warnings about unhandled enum values.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: ha_innodb.cc adds default: break; in innobase_lock_autoinc (after the mutex unlock case), start_stmt, and external_lock switch statements
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'There are also a few switch statements on enum values in the handler code that are missing default cases, which produces compiler warnings.' [3: Logical Necessity] Missing default cases on enum switches cause compiler warnings that pollute the build output. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must add the missing default cases to the identified switch statements.

title: 1.11: Replaces thd_get_thread_id() calls with direct THD::thread_id member access for logging and INFORMATION_SCHEMA population in InnoDB code

annotations.description: Verifies that calls to the service API wrapper thd_get_thread_id() are replaced with direct access to the THD::thread_id member for populating INFORMATION_SCHEMA.INNODB_TRX and for WSREP debug logging throughout InnoDB.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: trx0i_s.cc replaces thd_get_thread_id(trx->mysql_thd) with trx->mysql_thd->thread_id; ha_innodb.cc replaces thd_get_thread_id in write_row, update_row, delete_row, wsrep_append_key, wsrep_append_keys, lock_wait_wsrep_kill with ->thread_id; lock0lock.cc replaces thd_get_thread_id(v->mysql_thd) with v->mysql_thd->thread_id
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the code goes through wrappers for 'getting the thread ID' and asks to make THD access more direct. [3: Logical Necessity] thd_get_thread_id() is a service API wrapper that adds unnecessary indirection for in-process code. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must replace thd_get_thread_id() calls with direct thread_id member access.

title: 1.12: Removes the extern C declaration block containing thd_*() prototypes from ha_innodb.h

annotations.description: Verifies that the extern "C" block in ha_innodb.h that declared service API wrapper prototypes (such as thd_non_transactional_update, thd_binlog_format, thd_binlog_filter_ok, thd_sqlcom_can_generate_row_events, thd_is_strict_mode) is removed, since these declarations are no longer needed after the wrapper calls are replaced with direct THD member access.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: ha_innodb.h removes the entire extern "C" { ... } block containing 5 function declarations (lines 549-525 in the old file)
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states declarations are 'scattered across ha_innodb.h and ha_prototypes.h' and asks that 'unnecessary wrappers and their declarations are removed.' [3: Logical Necessity] Keeping dead declarations for removed wrappers would leave misleading artifacts in the header. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must remove the extern C declaration block from ha_innodb.h.

title: 1.13: Removes all four obsolete thd_*() forward declarations from ha_prototypes.h (thd_has_edited_nontrans_tables, thd_charset, thd_trx_is_read_only, thd_trx_is_auto_commit)

annotations.description: Verifies that forward declarations for the removed wrapper functions are cleaned up from ha_prototypes.h, including thd_has_edited_nontrans_tables, thd_charset, thd_trx_is_read_only, and thd_trx_is_auto_commit.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: ha_prototypes.h removes declarations for thd_has_edited_nontrans_tables (with comment block), thd_charset, thd_trx_is_read_only (with comment block), thd_trx_is_auto_commit (with comment block)
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states declarations are 'scattered across ha_innodb.h and ha_prototypes.h' and asks that 'unnecessary wrappers and their declarations are removed.' [3: Logical Necessity] Declarations for removed functions would cause linker errors or dead code confusion. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must remove the obsolete declarations from ha_prototypes.h.

title: 1.14: Completes InnoDB thd_*() cleanup by replacing every remaining service API wrapper in scope for this refactor with direct THD member access or local macros in handler, row, and trx code paths

annotations.description: Verifies that the remaining thd_*() service API wrapper calls beyond the ones explicitly named in the prompt are also replaced with direct THD member access or equivalent local macros. This covers thd_query_string, thd_get_xid, thd_wakeup_subsequent_commits, thd_tablespace_op, thd_sql_command, thd_test_options, thd_tx_isolation, thd_in_lock_tables, and thd_log_warnings.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: ha_innodb.cc replaces extern declarations of thd_get_query_id/thd_clear_error with local #define macros for thd_get_query_id, thd_in_lock_tables, thd_log_warnings, thd_sql_command, thd_test_options, thd_tx_isolation; ha_innodb.cc replaces thd_get_xid() with thd->get_xid(), thd_wakeup_subsequent_commits() with thd->wakeup_subsequent_commits(), thd_tablespace_op() with thd->tablespace_op; ha_innodb.cc replaces thd_query_string() with thd->query_string in innobase_get_stmt_unsafe(); row0ins.cc replaces thd_sql_command(thd) with thd->lex->sql_command; trx0trx.cc replaces thd_sql_command() with thd->lex->sql_command
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the code goes through wrappers for 'many other basic operations' beyond the named five, and asks to 'clean up this indirection throughout the InnoDB and RocksDB storage engine code.' [3: Logical Necessity] Leaving some wrapper calls unconverted while converting others would produce inconsistent code style and incomplete refactoring. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must replace all remaining thd_*() wrapper calls with direct THD access throughout the codebase.

title: 4.1: Modifies any test files or testing logic in any way

annotations.description: Detects if the agent modifies any files in the mysql-test directory, any *_test.cc files, or any other testing infrastructure, which is explicitly prohibited by the prompt constraints.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: modifies only non-test source files: sql/sql_class.cc, storage/innobase/dict/dict0crea.cc, storage/innobase/dict/dict0dict.cc, storage/innobase/eval/eval0eval.cc, storage/innobase/handler/ha_innodb.cc, storage/innobase/handler/ha_innodb.h, storage/innobase/handler/handler0alter.cc, storage/innobase/include/eval0eval.inl, storage/innobase/include/ha_prototypes.h, storage/innobase/include/trx0i_s.h, storage/innobase/include/trx0trx.h, storage/innobase/lock/lock0lock.cc, storage/innobase/row/row0ins.cc, storage/innobase/row/row0sel.cc, storage/innobase/row/row0uins.cc, storage/innobase/trx/trx0i_s.cc, storage/innobase/trx/trx0purge.cc, storage/innobase/trx/trx0trx.cc, storage/rocksdb/ha_rocksdb.cc. No files under mysql-test/ or any test files are modified.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly and emphatically prohibits modifying test files. [3: Logical Necessity] Modifying tests could mask regressions or cause false test failures. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must NOT modify any test files or testing logic.

title: 4.2: Removes thd_has_edited_nontrans_tables() functionality without preserving equivalent nontransactional-table-edit detection for the deadlock victim selection

annotations.description: Detects if the agent removes the thd_has_edited_nontrans_tables() helper function without ensuring that the deadlock detector in lock0lock.cc still has access to equivalent nontransactional-table-edit information, which is critical for correct deadlock victim selection.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch: ha_innodb.cc removes thd_has_edited_nontrans_tables(); lock0lock.cc adds a static replacement in the Deadlock namespace that directly checks thd->transaction->all.modified_non_trans_table
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] The deadlock detector uses nontransactional-table-edit status to avoid rolling back transactions that have made non-reversible changes to MyISAM or other non-transactional tables. Removing this check without replacement would cause the deadlock detector to make suboptimal victim choices, potentially losing non-transactional data. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must NOT remove the nontransactional-table-edit check without preserving equivalent functionality for deadlock detection.
